channel.title: Sliding Window on Tech Tutorials
channel.description: Recent content in Sliding Window on Tech Tutorials

item.title: Fixed Size Window
item.description: A sliding window approach where the window size remains constant throughout the iteration. Commonly used for problems like maximum sum subarray of size k or average of subarrays.

item.title: Variable Size Window
item.description: A flexible sliding window where the size expands or shrinks based on a given condition. Useful for problems like longest substring or subarray satisfying a constraint.

item.title: Variable Size Window Plus Hashmap
item.description: Enhances variable windows by using a hash map to track element frequency or presence. Essential for solving problems like longest substring without repeating characters or anagram matching.

item.title: Window Plus Monotonic Deque
item.description: Combines sliding window with a deque that maintains elements in sorted order (monotonic). Used to efficiently find maximum or minimum values in a window in O(n) time.
